summary: Latvia's posture is carried by CERT.LV, the national computer security incident response team, which engages with both post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution and frames quantum-safe infrastructure within the European EuroQCI initiative. Its Q-Ready conference brings together work on quantum-safe security, but no national migration timeline or standards selection has been published.
govActors: CERT.LV | Latvian national computer security incident response team, hosts the Q-Ready conference and events on quantum-safe cryptography
provenance: CERT.LV lead, PQC and QKD, EuroQCI framing: Q-Ready 25 conference announcement (ingested)

Survey of PQC methods (lattice-based NTRU/LWE, code-based McEliece, multivariate): presentation hosted on CERT.LV events area, authored by an academic researcher rather than CERT.LV, flagged for review (ingested, T4)
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp (NIST/ETSI/ISO) named as national guidance in captured summaries
algorithms families named only inside an academic survey, not adopted as Latvian guidance, so standardFamilies/algorithms omitted - REVIEW
hybridDeployment/migrationTimeline/targetCompletion omitted: not stated in captured summaries
